title: Species-specific quantitative PCR (qPCR) protocol for Haplosporidium pinnae detection
author: Monserrat López-Sanmartín
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xe7fjhn

steps.title: The qPCR assay for H
steps.instruction: The qPCR assay for H. pinnae detection and quantification was carried out in duplicate using the species-specific primer pair (HpF3/HpR3) in a Mx3000P Thermocycler (Agilent).

steps.title: Amplification reactions were performed in a...
steps.instruction: Amplification reactions were performed in a total volume of 10 µl comprising:

steps.title: 5 µl of PowerUpTM SYBRTM Green Master Mix (Applied Biosystems),
steps.instruction: 5 µl of PowerUpTM SYBRTM Green Master Mix (Applied Biosystems),

steps.title: Negative control (without DNA or with H
steps.instruction: Negative control (without DNA or with H. pinnae non-infected fan mussel DNA, previously confirmed by qPCR),

steps.title: positive controls (samples with positive cPCR amplification for H
steps.instruction: positive controls (samples with positive cPCR amplification for H. pinnae) and

steps.title: standard curve (plasmid HpF3/HpR3) were included in each qPCR assay
steps.instruction: standard curve (plasmid HpF3/HpR3) were included in each qPCR assay.

steps.title: After testing various annealing temperature, the...
steps.instruction: After testing various annealing temperature, the final qPCR program was: 1 cycle for 2 min at 50ºC; 1 cycle for 2 min at 95ºC, 40 cycles of amplification at 95ºC for 15 s, 60ºC for 18 s and 72ºC for 1 min and followed by a dissociation stage for determining the melt curve (Quantitative SYBR Green with dissociation curve).

steps.title: Two standard curves were calculated using...
steps.instruction: Two standard curves were calculated using serial dilutions of the plasmid and plasmid diluted with DNA of fan mussel;

steps.title: the efficiency (E) was from the...
steps.instruction: the efficiency (E) was from the slope of the standard curve following formula [11] E=10-1/slope-1.

steps.title: Melting curve was generated with temperatures increments of 0
steps.instruction: Melting curve was generated with temperatures increments of 0.5ºC s-1 starting at 60ºC and ending at 95ºC in order to ensure that a single PCR product was amplified for the primers.

steps.title: Amplification was also confirmed by electrophoresis...
steps.instruction: Amplification was also confirmed by electrophoresis on 2% agarose gels, in 1% TAE (Tris acetate EDTA buffer), stained with Red Safe (Nitron Biotechnology) and scanned in a GelDoc-It Imagen System Ultraviolet Transilluminator (BioImaging Systems).
